skill.name: security-review
skill.description: Use this skill when adding authentication, handling user input, working with secrets, creating API endpoints, or implementing payment/sensitive features. Provides comprehensive security checklist and patterns.
skill.summary: Comprehensive security review skill providing checklists and patterns for secure code development.
skill.author: affaan-m

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 90 potential security issues, but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file is an educational security checklist document written in Markdown, not executable code. Pattern-based detection flagged code examples (in fenced code blocks) as if they were actual runtime behavior. The skill teaches secure coding practices including secrets management, input validation, SQL injection prevention, XSS/CSRF protection, and authentication patterns. No actual external commands, network requests, or sensitive file access occur - these are documentation examples only.

content.value_statement: Developers need to ensure their code follows security best practices but often miss critical vulnerabilities. This skill provides comprehensive checklists and patterns to identify and fix security issues before deployment.
actual_capabilities: Provides comprehensive security checklist covering 10 critical areas including secrets management, input validation, and SQL injection prevention
Shows secure code patterns for authentication, authorization, and JWT token handling
Demonstrates XSS and CSRF protection techniques with working examples
Includes rate limiting patterns for API endpoints
Covers sensitive data handling in logs and error messages
Provides blockchain-specific security patterns for Solana wallet verification
limitations: This skill provides educational guidance only - it does not automatically scan or fix code
Security patterns are framework-specific (Next.js, Supabase, Express) and may need adaptation
Does not replace professional security audits or penetration testing

use_cases.title: Pre-deployment security review
use_cases.description: Run this skill before deploying any feature that handles user data, authentication, or payments to identify potential vulnerabilities.

use_cases.title: Security-focused code review
use_cases.description: Use during pull request reviews to ensure new code follows security best practices and doesn't introduce vulnerabilities.
use_cases.target_user: Tech leads and senior developers reviewing team code

use_cases.title: Learning secure coding patterns
use_cases.description: Study the provided examples to understand common vulnerabilities and their secure implementations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic security checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my code for security vulnerabilities using the security-review skill checklist. Check for hardcoded secrets, input validation, and proper error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick security pass on new feature code

prompt_templates.title: Authentication implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm implementing user authentication with JWT tokens. Use the security-review skill to show me the correct patterns for token storage, authorization checks, and session management.

prompt_templates.title: API endpoint security audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my API endpoint for security issues. Check rate limiting, input validation, SQL injection prevention, and proper authorization. Here's my code: [paste code]
prompt_templates.scenario: Securing a new REST or GraphQL endpoint

prompt_templates.title: Full pre-deployment security review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete security review before production deployment. Go through all 10 sections of the security checklist: secrets management, input validation, SQL injection, authentication, XSS, CSRF, rate limiting, data exposure, blockchain security (if applicable), and dependency security. Report any issues found with specific fixes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Final security check before releasing to production

output_examples.input: Review this login endpoint for security issues
output_examples.output:
Security review results:

1. PASS: No hardcoded secrets found - using environment variables correctly
2. FAIL: Missing input validation on email/password fields - add Zod schema validation
3. PASS: Using parameterized queries - no SQL injection risk
4. FAIL: Token stored in localStorage - move to httpOnly cookie
5. PASS: Rate limiting configured on endpoint
6. FAIL: Error messages expose 'user not found' vs 'wrong password' - use generic message

Recommended fixes provided for each failing item.

output_examples.input: How do I safely handle file uploads?
output_examples.output:
Secure file upload pattern:

1. Size validation: Limit to 5MB maximum
2. Type validation: Whitelist specific MIME types (image/jpeg, image/png)
3. Extension validation: Check file extension matches allowed list
4. Storage: Save outside web root with randomized filename
5. Sanitization: Scan for malware if handling user uploads

Example implementation with all checks included.

best_practices: Always use environment variables for secrets - never hardcode API keys, passwords, or tokens in source code
Validate all user input with schemas before processing - use libraries like Zod for type-safe validation
Store authentication tokens in httpOnly cookies with SameSite=Strict, never in localStorage
anti_patterns: Hardcoding secrets in source code or committing .env files to version control
Storing JWT tokens in localStorage where they are vulnerable to XSS attacks
Exposing detailed error messages or stack traces to users instead of generic messages

faq.question: When should I activate this security review skill?
faq.answer: Activate when implementing authentication, handling user input, creating API endpoints, working with secrets, implementing payments, or before any production deployment.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically scan my code for vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides educational checklists and patterns. You manually review your code against the provided security criteria and examples.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: Examples focus on Next.js, Express, and Supabase, but the security principles apply universally across web frameworks.

faq.question: How do I check if my .env file is properly excluded from git?
faq.answer: Run 'git ls-files | grep env' to verify .env files are not tracked. Ensure .env.local is in your .gitignore file.

faq.question: Is parameterized query enough to prevent all SQL injection?
faq.answer: Parameterized queries prevent most SQL injection, but also validate input types, use ORMs correctly, and apply least-privilege database permissions.

faq.question: What should I do if npm audit finds vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: Run 'npm audit fix' for automatic fixes. For remaining issues, review the advisory and update affected packages. Never ignore critical vulnerabilities.
